Now talking about the Chicken burger.First of all,the buns were stale,they crumbled like dried leaves when we tried eating.At the end,it was just a big mush of bread crumbs and patty.Secondly, the chicken patty was flavourless and the size of it was ridiculous(just look at the pic).
Three of us went there,came back totally disappointed.Its not worth visit and influencers totally exaggerate this place.

I had the worst burger of my life here. It was dry as a desert. The layer of sauce was just a few millimeters wide. The slice of cucumber in it was very thick, and the bun itself was so oversized that every bite felt like a struggle. On the other hand, the choila momo felt like it had sand particles while chewing. Also, they had an overpriced water bottle that had to be purchased if we wanted to drink. I genuinely lose respect for restaurants that don’t offer basic drinking water to their customers.

Needs Improvement

The food definitely needs a lot of improvement. The platter we ordered was below average—the momo and potatoes tasted stale, which was disappointing.

I’m giving 2 stars solely for the coffee, as it was the only thing I enjoyed.

This place has ample amount of sitting space. Different kind of good food are available here. Service here should be improved. The staff weren’t responding properly. Overall food was satisfactory. Parking is available here. Thank you.

Very very average. The food was average, although the buff momo was good (we came specifically for the momo because there was a discount on it haha). The service took way too long, even more than average on other restaurants. The burger did not have a good taste and they don't even provide water in the restaurant! Hope the food and service improves from here on out because it was a painfully mediocre experience.
It is a nice place. The food was good except momo. I don’t have a pic of momo but it was not good at all.

This place has a really nice and vibrant vibe, but the food was not worth the price. I ordered buff momo and sukuti thukpa. The momo didn’t have enough filling, and the meat tasted bad. The thukpa was Dharan-style and felt more like dal than thukpa. Overall, the ambiance was good, but the food quality needs improvement.
Everything was good from service to the food we ordered. Must try and must visit place. Affordable budget friendly place for everyone.

The place is good, has space and good for gathering but the food 😔disappointing, dull and bland. The momo had zero fillings (just like her feelings for you). Tasted weird and had too much fat. There was no meat inside. Overall a very mid experience and not worth for the price .
overall ok, 1stly parking problem and washroom is side by side with dining thats lokks unusual

Great Pizza and Great drinks.
The pan pizza is really great and tasty. The crust is crunchy at bottom and soft at middle of pizza. The toppings are well seasoned and tasty. The drinks are cheap
(i.e Rs 190 for hot chocolate).
The ambience is good as well. They have alot of parking space. Popcorn chicken qaa great as well

Pizza World is a great spot if you’re looking for a quiet place to relax or study. They have both indoor and outdoor seating, and even offer books you can read while enjoying your meal. The staff is super friendly, and the food is absolutely delicious—especially the pizza! Highly recommended for anyone who wants good food in a peaceful setting.

Great place with a cozy vibe! The pizza was really good, fresh, flavorful, and satisfying. I also tried a few different items from the menu, and everything was consistently tasty. Definitely worth a visit if you’re craving a good meal in a relaxed setting.

Best pizza I have had in a while. We tried the meat lover's pizza and I highly recommend it. Full of toppings and cheese. Ample parking space available for both two wheelers and four wheelers. The place is not as expensive as it seems from the outside. Good atmosphere with amazing hospitality. Definitely coming back here again.
Very impressive and cosy atmosphere, great food.

We organized our consultancy's farewell and teej program overall, budget friendly,and the food was also good. The staffs were polite, well groomed and helpful. Will again visit soon and recommend others too.

Solid Breakfast Spot

Visited for breakfast recently and had a decent American breakfast—nothing extraordinary, but it hit the spot. The eggs, toast, and sausages were cooked well.

Big plus: plenty of parking space, which made the visit hassle-free. Service was okay, though a bit impersonal.
A practical spot for a quick morning meal if you’re in the area, but don’t expect anything memorable.
Good place. Good for holding functions and parties as. Pricing is little high for food items. Atmosphere is also good. Different halls are available for larger groups as well.
Good Restaurant. With lots of chain. Staff are friendly. Food is great. Price a bit on the higher side.
The food was so good here. The staffs were also friendly so service was top class. For atmosphere the place was little small. They had so many menus and each foods were very delicious.

Excellent Newari food. Went there for lunch today, after meal we were asked for our feedback and in reply we told them to open a branch in Lalitpur.
Must visit if you like Newari cuisine.

Tha Chhen Newari Cuisine is best for local Newari food. If you like newari dining, you should visit this specific restaurant which is located at mid Baneshwor. All the food was delicious but Buff Choila and Buff Sukiti was most tastier. The staffs were also welcoming and offered you food gently with best suggestions. Also the price of food is very reasonable.
Highly recommended to visit here for typically Newari Taste.

Our experience at Tha Chhen Restaurant was a mixed bag. While the food was undoubtedly good, the same cannot be said for the service we received.

Unfortunately, our encounter with the waiter left much to be desired. Not only did he initially bring the wrong order to our table, but he also failed to comprehend our subsequent requests, resulting in yet another incorrect dish being served.

Moreover, when we politely pointed out the mistake, the waiter's response was far from courteous. It seemed as though he was in a rush to “push us” out of the restaurant, which left us feeling unwelcome and disappointed.

Despite the delectable food, the overall dining experience was marred by the poor service. Tha Cheen Restaurant may have good food, but they certainly need to work on improving their customer service to match the quality of their cuisine.
The food is really good with authentic newari taste.
If you're on the hunt for the best Saphu Mhicha in the town , Tha:chhen is the place to be. Newari cuisine at its best 👌

I heard about this cafe a lot and decided to go with my friend , we ordered butter chicken masala and butter naan, butter chicken masala was pretty good, The taste was up to the mark but I have a bit concern about it's food colour cause it was too bold and didn't even fade after washing hand with soap , but overall it was good ...

And my friend was having problems while breathing cause there was no separate area for smoking and people were smoking frequently...

Firstly this place have enough parking space. I went through c momo n chicken chowmein n chowmein was good, c momo was also not bad but the ketchup added in it was too sweet other wish the crisp taste of momo was good. Overall, service was pretty good.

Ambience is good but a bit outdated decorations. For me I think the price is a bit expensive in comparison to other places. And I think they didn't use good ketchup in my c momo. Janeko momo was good. Too crowd on saturdays.

foods were good in taste with nice simple presentation. but could've been better.
feels abit overpriced according to quantity.
had a home packing of Kothey MoMo as well which wasn't well packed and wasn't good as expected.

The only good thing about the place is, it has good parking.
Asked the waiter to clear the dirty plate from the table, and I was completely ignored. I then ordered C veg momo but got served steam momo. Waiting was almost an hour for momo and 30 mins for tea while the cafe was quiet.
Food over is really good. It need some music that will create great ambience. Definitely a place in New Baneshwor to try out inside C&M building. Quality of food is really exceptional.

Luxurious ambience. Delicious foods.
Especially pressure cooker mo:mo.

Service was really slow even there was no one literally no one in the restro.. Plus we ordered only two items which took them almost 1 hour 15 mins and the chowmin which they bought straight away from kitchen (as per the waiter) was freaking cold. Do not waste your time and money in this restaurant
Worst restaurant in baneshwor ,
Very good place and very good service more than that food is awesome👍

This place is truly the best for Laphing and Keema Noodles. The dishes are freshly prepared, and the taste doesn’t disappoint. Mix Laphing is the best here, in my opinion. Also, their Momo and Thulo Momo are equally good. The only downside is that, since they make their food fresh after the order, the waiting time can be longer—especially for Momo and Shyaphale, up to 30 minutes when there is a crowd, so do keep that in mind. Other than that, this place doesn’t disappoint.

Gaga's is one of my  top favourite places for keema noodles..It tastes delicious and satisfies your craving. The atmosphere is nice and the service is okayy.. but every time i go there, the quantity of keema noodles gets less and gets more expensive.. Nevertheless, i would still recommend to visit this place..

We had:
1. Mix Laphing Jhol

One of the worst laphings we've ever had. Tasted salty, and the soy sauce wasn't good either.

The place is decently spacious but has no tissue papers on the table.

They were on my bucket list for so long, and finally got the chance to be there but the taste didn't meet my expectations. The chips used in dry laphing were not crispy enough, so it ruined the whole taste of the laphing whereas the egg veg noodles tasted average.

It is a well-known vibrant hub for Tibetan food, so it's packed with customers at peak hours. A good place with a pleasant ambiance to enjoy some authentic keema noodles. The keema noodles, paired with their extra spicy sauce, are definitely worth a try. However, the laphing here can be bland at times. The Jhol laphing was a bit disappointing. With a variety of food options to choose from, it is definitely a go-to place for a meal when you are in the Baneswor area. Will definitely visit again.

Ordered from Pathao and was super hungry after work at 2:45 am. I got the order but totally messed up with their taste yr. I literally cannot express how much frustrated I was with their taste when I was so hungry at the same time. I ate fried rice, which was too oily, and the boiled corn was fine, but that potato, I literally felt like beating that damn chef, yaar.

And that chatpate, OMG! It felt like eating crushed bhoja with tomatoes, and that jhol was just colored warm water 🤬. I lost my Rs.500 on that horrible taste.

I seriously request the owners: please throw that chef out if you don’t want your restaurant to shut down soon.

I have no complaints with the service and ambience because the ambience is so pretty and classy. As well as the service is too good.

But the taste of this masala tea I had is tasteless. Also, this chatpate in the picture looks fancy but overpriced for this taste. Just average taste.

I always admire this place but literally they should work on these things.

If you love to eat chatpate this can be a unique experience for you. The jhol chatpate was quite delicious being spicy and tangy at the same time. But the uniqueness was the texture after adding the jhol. In addition loved the sadheko momo and cold chicken here as well. However, the milk tea was wayyyy too sweet. The ambience would say welcoming and the menu vast.

Interior decoration is good but your food quality not in prompt  lets be honest and say worst

Momo are over cooked , possible frozen , taste is negative like

Music choice guys watch up

The first time I went to this place, we found hair in the momo achaar. Then, the second time I went to this place, there was a small cockroach in my chicken sandwich. This place is very unhygienic. If I could, I wouldn't even give this place 1 star. Disgusting. Regret giving this place a second chance.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
The place has such a cozy, warm, and peaceful vibe perfect if you want a quiet, relaxed dining experience.

As for the food, it’s definitely not bad… well prepared and nicely presented but the flavors are quite subtle. If you’re used to bold, spicy, or strong Asian flavors, it might not fully hit your taste buds. This spot feels more suited for those who enjoy classic European/French-style cuisine and mild seasoning.

Overall, great ambiance and experience, but food-wise it depends on your palate.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Wow wow wow. While staying at Babar Mahal Vilas, I walked through this restaurant on numerous occasions, saving my visit for this restaurant. Omg. The service and fast menu of food delivery was next level. Having travelled the world, there has been no better service than here in Nepal, ever. Absolutely amazing delivery. 5⭐️.
Also it looked like rain, & within a minute, they erected a huge sail to cover the entire open air diners inside a minute. 🤷‍♂️, amazing experience.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: The staff at Chez Caroline have perfected the art of hospitality and making a guest feel at home. I've been staying at the hotel the restaurant is adjacent to, and have eaten here for the majority of my two week stay in Kathmandu. Although the food is fantastic, our favorite part is definitely the company. My friend and I have come to know Madhu and the rest of the staff as such incredibly kind and thoughtful people. They go out of their way to ensure you're satisfied every step of the way. It has become my favorite way to end a long day. Madhu's kindness and warmth is radiating. I only regret not being able to dine here every night of our stay. There's several things I'll miss about Nepal once I return home, Madhu and Chez Caroline is certainly at the top of the list. Thank you for the great food, great service, and great memories.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
A nice old reputed place to try French cuisine. It was buzzing with tourists from France or who could speak French. Babar Mahal is a quaint place with Rana culture & does have queer expensive stores.
Food not bad, the chicken dish passable, salad was good but service great.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: Food was great, but all the stars go to our server Madhu, the nicest francophone man to ever exist. His attention and recommendations made the experience so enjoyable.

we ordered 3 bowls of pork thukpa, 1 plate chicken steam momo and 1 plate alu chop, Don't know about momo and alu chop, because it was already too late.

The pork in pork thukpa we orders was not cleaned properly and  the food was not that warm. The pork meat in the thukpa still had the hair from the pork's skin.

When we asked to talk to the cook the manager stated that cook cannot come and talk.

I would not recommend this place for anything.

Went there to celebrate a birthday party. At first, the place felt so empty that we had second thoughts, but it turned out to be a nice experience.

The food was good, and we didn’t face any issues or negative experiences. A quiet spot if you're looking for a peaceful celebration.
Cozy vibe and flavorful dishes, with their signature Dharane pork thukpa standing out as a hearty, must-try comfort bowl.

I didn't have much expectation from them. Only went because my friend saw a yum looking Thukpa. But that was the least tasty among the small sets we ordered. Chicken Sekuwa was better than Thukpa. Best was the Matka Cooler. Overall unexpectedly good food with an ok enough ambience.
Pork dharne thukpa is best.good for celebration.. best time at parpala with delicious nangloset

Newa Lahana in New Road, Kathmandu is a wonderful place to experience authentic Newari cuisine in a warm and traditional setting. The indoor ambience feels cozy and welcoming, with both table seating and traditional mat-style seating available, giving guests a cultural dining experience. The restaurant can accommodate small as well as larger groups, making it suitable for family gatherings or group outings. There is also a large bar area, adding to the lively atmosphere.

Newari cuisine is known for its rich flavors, variety of textures, and balanced combination of spices. It often includes beaten rice (chiura), lentils, spiced meats, fermented items, pickles, and traditional beverages. The Samaya Baji set is a must-try, offering a complete platter that represents traditional Newari food culture. Meat lovers will enjoy dishes like choila, kachila, sapu micha, and sukuti, all packed with bold and authentic flavors. Other highlights include momo, bara (wo), and chattamari, which are staples of Newar cuisine. For dessert, yomari is absolutely mouth-watering and a perfect sweet ending.

They also serve traditional alcoholic aila and white beer, along with a variety of other alcoholic drinks, juices, and coffee. Parking around New Road can be difficult, so planning ahead is recommended. Payments are convenient with cash, cards, and FonePay accepted, and VAT bills are provided. Overall, Newa Lahana is a great place to explore the true taste of Newari heritage.

They have been Opened for a Year now.
Foodwise-
Buff Choilla 9/10
Buff Sekuwa 3/10(thats not hiw a sekuwa is done)
Veg Momo 7/10( was freshly made and not frozen)
Jibro 9/10 very well made.
Chyanng 8/10
Aila 8/10

The Staffs were attentive and nice.
The Ambiance is good accentuated by the Old House they have.
The Toilets are Clean and well maintained.
The food was so authentic and full of flavor. Top notch quality and quantity with affordable price. Enrich with flavor, every bite feels worthy. This place is must to visit when you are at ktm.

This place is a GEM! Great service! The best I’ve had in four months in Nepal with a good selection of traditional Newari cuisine for reasonable prices. Outside the place is disorganized with the hustle and bustle of the streets. But inside it’s an island of order, beauty and grace. I had several people fighting to give me service and I got my food quite quickly. It’s not anything I’ve had before, and as someone who manly likes Thai and Chinese I found it distant from that. But it was a great new experience and I’ll come again! Very unique Newari dishes that are different from the normal Indian food, momo and takali. Highly recommend! It would be great with a group.

The food was absolutely amazing and so tasty!
Everyone should definitely try traditional Newari food at Newa Lahana, Newroad.
The flavors were rich, authentic, and truly unforgettable.
Highly recommended for anyone wanting to experience real Newari cuisine!

I had a great experience at this restaurant! The **momo soup was absolutely the best**—flavorful, comforting, and perfectly cooked. The mutton biriyani is a must-try as well; rich, aromatic, and incredibly satisfying. The **ambience and music were really good**, creating a relaxed and enjoyable dining atmosphere. Definitely worth a visit!

Graveaty Restaurant & Bar is a true gem in Thamel, Kathmandu.
We visited for lunch, and although it’s tucked away in a maze of streets, it’s easy to find using Google Maps.

The menu offers a great mix of Indian, Nepalese, and modern fast food, along with a wide range of both alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks. The first floor is a comfortable space where you can eat, drink, and smoke.

The food was excellent, and the service made the experience even better. The waitress, Mamata, took great care of us and was very welcoming.

I would definitely come back to try more dishes. A solid 5-star experience.

Had a very full lunch today. Tasty food, great ambience with chill music 🎵 🎶
Good service, the staff explained to me how to eat Vege Thali, now I know the yoghurt and ghee are enemies, don't mix it together 😊. Love this restaurant and will recommend to my friends in Malaysia.

Contemporary designed restaurant with modern interior. We ordered the chicken Jhol Mo:Mo as well as the steamed Buff Mo:Mo with some chopped vegetables. Both dishes were very tasty, warm and nicely plated. The staff was super friendly and English speaking. We paid 1130 NPR for two dishes and two teas.

Had a wonderful experience at this café in Kathmandu Thamel. The food was delicious,fresh and full of flavor and the location is perfect—centrally placed and easy to reach.Beautiful ambiance, peaceful vibes and great hospitality.A perfect spot to relax and enjoy good food in the heart of the city...

I ordered the wings, a burger, and a chocolate beverage. Overall, the food was okay, but there were a few things that stood out. The hot spicy wings (level 3) were unexpectedly sweet, the flavor felt unbalanced, and the sauce tasted overwhelmingly like ketchup, even if it wasn’t. The burger looked promising, but the taste was dominated by mayo, which overshadowed everything else.

The seating situation was also not ideal. It felt crowded, and the single tables were noticeably too low for the chairs, making it uncomfortable to sit and eat.
Worst burger i ever had just waste of money and time. Bacon was too smelly and fried chicken in burger was too cold and bun was not fresh.

Anyone who like spice will love ordering spicy chicken here.
a compact place, you might feel noisy because it’s right beside new-road.

The food was 8.5/10. The wings were on point spicy and none spicy both. The spicy burger was one of the best I ever had but the none spicy lacked flavours. Service was fast and meals were fresh. The place is small, and might not accommodate everyone during peak hours. The place is clean.

Worst food and service. The burger we had was disgusting. The buns were so cold, like they forgot to heat them up, and it took more than 20 minutes just to make the cold burger. Never eating there again.

Revolution Café was busy but not overly crowded when we visited. It has a very extensive menu covering Western favourites alongside Nepali, other Asian dishes, pizzas, Mexican food, and Italian pasta. The food and drinks were good, though a little slow to arrive.

Seating is available both indoors and outdoors. The outdoor tables are right on the street and can be a bit loud, but still able to hold a conversation without issue.

Service was very friendly and welcoming.

They mentioned that vegetables and fruit are washed with mineral water and that organic ingredients are used where possible.

Cards are accepted, but there’s a 4% surcharge, so paying cash is cheaper.

Toilets are Western-style and located just outside the restaurant.

Absolutely loved *Revolution Caffe* in Kathmandu! ★★★★★
The food is incredibly delicious — full of flavor, fresh, and made with care. The menu has great variety, and everything we tried was outstanding. The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, and the staff is super friendly and attentive.

Highly recommended if you’re in Kathmandu and looking for a tasty meal in a chill vibe. Definitely one of our favorite food spots in the city!

Food here was so good. This cafe offers variety of foods with good taste. Huge sitting space is available here. Service was fast and good. Indoor and outdoor both sitting space is available here. Ambiance is also good. You may feel the price is little expensive but food is good. Thank you.

I visited the restaurant for lunch on a Sunday afternoon. It was a pleasant experience overall even though the windows were open with an aircooler.As we neared the end of our meal, the place got quite crowded, which seemed to slow down the service a bit—likely due to the sudden rush. Still, the staff remained friendly and courteous throughout.

The Chicken Tikka Masala was absolutely delicious and paired perfectly with the homemade chapati. The lemonade was refreshing and well-balanced.

However, the BBQ Kathmandu was disappointing and not something I would order again. Food and Beverage service was definitely little too slow for my liking as we had plans

Despite that, I would certainly return to this place for another meal
The price of foods are justified by the quantity they offer. Carbonara and potato sticks was best among our orders.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
The place has such a cozy, warm, and peaceful vibe perfect if you want a quiet, relaxed dining experience.

As for the food, it’s definitely not bad… well prepared and nicely presented but the flavors are quite subtle. If you’re used to bold, spicy, or strong Asian flavors, it might not fully hit your taste buds. This spot feels more suited for those who enjoy classic European/French-style cuisine and mild seasoning.

Overall, great ambiance and experience, but food-wise it depends on your palate.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Wow wow wow. While staying at Babar Mahal Vilas, I walked through this restaurant on numerous occasions, saving my visit for this restaurant. Omg. The service and fast menu of food delivery was next level. Having travelled the world, there has been no better service than here in Nepal, ever. Absolutely amazing delivery. 5⭐️.
Also it looked like rain, & within a minute, they erected a huge sail to cover the entire open air diners inside a minute. 🤷‍♂️, amazing experience.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: The staff at Chez Caroline have perfected the art of hospitality and making a guest feel at home. I've been staying at the hotel the restaurant is adjacent to, and have eaten here for the majority of my two week stay in Kathmandu. Although the food is fantastic, our favorite part is definitely the company. My friend and I have come to know Madhu and the rest of the staff as such incredibly kind and thoughtful people. They go out of their way to ensure you're satisfied every step of the way. It has become my favorite way to end a long day. Madhu's kindness and warmth is radiating. I only regret not being able to dine here every night of our stay. There's several things I'll miss about Nepal once I return home, Madhu and Chez Caroline is certainly at the top of the list. Thank you for the great food, great service, and great memories.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
A nice old reputed place to try French cuisine. It was buzzing with tourists from France or who could speak French. Babar Mahal is a quaint place with Rana culture & does have queer expensive stores.
Food not bad, the chicken dish passable, salad was good but service great.

address: Tanka Prasad Ghumti Sadak, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: Food was great, but all the stars go to our server Madhu, the nicest francophone man to ever exist. His attention and recommendations made the experience so enjoyable.

I went here with my friend to try new taste after watching a food vlogger but the taste wasn’t good. We ate Spring roll and Pork Momo.
While the chicken had no taste in Spring Rolls the Momo was decent  and small according to the price.

Not a recommended place

Finally got to try New Dish in New Road, and it lived up to the hype! I had the pork thukpa and chicken spring rolls. The flavors were fantastic—not overly spiced, so you could really taste the meat and vegetables. The portions are huge; my thukpa could easily be shared by two people. The chicken spring roll was great. The ambiance isn't the best, but the food and nice service more than make up for it. A definite must-try for their pork dishes!

THE BEST PORK MOMO I’ve ever had.

Not quite sure about the spring roll though, caution: it’s a lot, much more filling than it seems.

Average service and ambience.

But quite expensive for just 8 pieces of momo.

New Food Restaurant, previously known as New Dish Restaurant, continues to be a go-to destination for food lovers, especially those with a craving for pork momo. Known for their melt-in-your-mouth pork momo, this dish remains a highlight that never fails to impress. The spring roll is another must-try item on the menu, highly popular among regular patrons.

What’s admirable is that despite the rebranding, the taste and quality have remained consistent over the years. The restaurant maintains a clean and decent ambiance, making it a comfortable place to dine in. Prices are reasonable, offering great value for the quality of food served.

Highly recommended for momo enthusiasts, New Food Restaurant guarantees a delightful culinary experience that keeps you coming back for more!

We tried the pork thukpa here, and while the portion size was decent for the price, I was disappointed with the meat. In most places, pork thukpa comes with crispy or well-cooked meat on top, but here it was just plain boiled meat-which really took away from the experience.

I also checked out the menu and found that many items...Another major letdown was the water situation. They only offer bottled water from a specific brand at a high cost. Why not provide regular jar water as an option? It feels like they're trying to inflate the bill with unnecessary extras, on top of already expensive food items.

Even though this place is popular and gets a lot of attention, the pricing just doesn't feel justified.  Sadly, it feels more like a business focused on profit than on customer
experience.
Nice coffee art and testy coffee best service and staff friendly thank you for biryani adda next time visit again
Food is super delicious and service is good too.well spent time…best for get together.
"Excellent quality biryani and authentic flavour and perfect spice balance. The presentation hygiene, service standards are highly recommended."❤️

The atmosphere is calm and clean, food is delicious as well, must try hydrabadi biryani if you’re extremely hungry and pressure cooker jhol Momo if you’re craving for tasty hot soup 🫶🏻. It’s a win, win to find a restaurant like this at a place like Newroad Gate. ✨

It has been a long time since I have this good coffee.
Latte art by Sanisha Magar 👍

Went again for Korean food and tried the combo and samgyeopsal 🔥
Grilling the meat ourselves was fun and a nice experience. The food was good overall, enjoyable vibes.
Bibimbap was okay, not bad, not amazing.
Nice place for a casual, fun Korean food experience. 😊
The food was delicious and the service was exceptionally good. Overall we had a great experience.

Food tastes good.
Staff service is great.
Parking nit available but road side parking for 2 wheelers available.
4 wheelers gotta park at nearby parking area and walk few minutes.
Loved the milkshake.

Our first lunch in Nepal!

While the starters were a bit underwhelming—the beans were too hard, and the bean sprouts were a tad too salty (perhaps a Nepalese preference, as the food here tends to be on the saltier side?)—the mains made up for it. They were flavorful and well-prepared.

It was also great to see a steady stream of diners, both locals and fellow travelers, which added to the lively atmosphere.

The coffee? Surprisingly decent and not too sweet!

Great food and great service. Staffs were very attentive and responsive to everything. They explained in detail about every order that we had confusions. Cheese tteokpokki was the best highlight!! Delicious 🤤

address: Thamel, Kwabahal-17, Inside Hotel Nepalaya, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Loved this place! It has a really nice atmosphere and is a great spot to relax with a beer. The food is delicious too — I had the chicken thukpa and it was absolutely tasty. Would definitely recommend!

address: Thamel, Kwabahal-17, Inside Hotel Nepalaya, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: The food is delicious! The views are incredible! And the staff is even better! Whenever I'm in Kathmandu this where I’ll enjoy a nice morning, afternoon or evening. They offer a great selection of western and Nepali options.

address: Thamel, Kwabahal-17, Inside Hotel Nepalaya, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Great 360° view of the Kathmandu Valley from the rooftop – perfect spot for sunset.
The staff is friendly and attentive.
We only had tea; it was okay, nothing special. The view was definitely the highlight of our visit

address: Thamel, Kwabahal-17, Inside Hotel Nepalaya, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: All the food was overly salty, and I don’t understand why the Google reviews are so high. This was the most disappointing meal I’ve had during my trip to Nepal.

address: Thamel, Kwabahal-17, Inside Hotel Nepalaya, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: The food was really good . Would go again . Loved everything we ordered .

address: P836+8G2 Sasa Complex, Khechapukhu Sadak, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Hidden gem - good spot for grabs and drinks. Good food and vibes just above the busy crowd.

address: P836+8G2 Sasa Complex, Khechapukhu Sadak, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Its a fun hang out spot and also some good picture spots above the ship .. may be baised on my openion of the few things i did taste but the food could be better, in my case the pizza was bad but amazing chicken so its better to ask for best sellers before ordering.

Over all the experience is awesome.

address: P836+8G2 Sasa Complex, Khechapukhu Sadak, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: The place is awesome with very comfortable seating options. Pretty empty when we visited the place. The food was average. The price is more on the higher side than other places. They however serve popcorn as a starter while waiting for your order. Overall average and okiesh place.

address: P836+8G2 Sasa Complex, Khechapukhu Sadak, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Just didn't like some food items. Otherwise it's a really cool place with good ambience and really good staffs and service. Would definitely recommend and yes I'll be going again.

address: P836+8G2 Sasa Complex, Khechapukhu Sadak, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Oh my goodness 😳......crowed and noisy place ma yesto ramro, slient ani chill place...soo good. They provide such a good quality food and reasonable price. Ekdam dami ambiance out door to indoor. Perfect for dates, gathering,  birthday party, & get together also,Family friendly environment.

What a wonderful place to visit! I found this restaurant judging the reviews from the Google and it was such a right decision! Went here two times to enjoy non-vegan set which is just an amazing option for dinner!

I highly recommend this place because it’s located in the city centre and you can feel nice atmosphere and the service was really great. And talking about the food, I think it’s one of the tastiest sets I ever tried in my life.

Thank you so much for such an incredible experience 😇 Mr. PRAKASH was my waiter and he was so respectful and nice to me. Highly appreciated that!

As soon as we walked in we were greeted and directed warmly. The staff were welcoming, friendly and helpful. The food took a while but music was nice and to be honest they had a bigger party inside.

As a foreign tourist the food was good - as a ethnic Nepali the khotey buff momo was absolutely fantastic. I had mutton biryani and buff khotey momo. I recommend the momo dish - especially for a light scran.

Would come again just for those dumplings alone. Price is pretty fair in my opinion.

Had got late for dinner and Google Maps threw up this place. Walked from  Rising Star Mall to Jasper. It was worth the walk.

Had one Thakali and it was well presented and with a good taste quotient. Dal, Bhaat with side dishes of Karela fries etc was perfect. Star of the show was Hog Plum pickle which was sweet n sour. This Hog Plum is home made by the owner herself.
Asheem Shreshtha who  attended me was very courteous and helpful

I went here mainly for the goat shank, and as I had to get rid of my last Rupees, I ordered some other dishes as well. I ordered the red house wine, on room temperature. They confirmed it was room temperature but it was freezing cold anyways. As starter I got the lemon coriander soup, which wasn't as expected. It was just a broth soup with vegetables and thicked with corn starch or so. Nothing to miss here. The chicken timur was boneless and better to eat, I liked that. The goat shank was really nice too, really tender and juicy and the vegetables on the side were crispy and fresh. To finish off I got the brownie with ice-cream. The brownie was dry and not really good. Overall an okeyish experience, but as they represent themselves as a higher class restaurant I expected better. 4 stars, but just on the edge. They should do something about the smoking people while people are dining.

Great food, nice service too!
The food was ready in the blink of an eye. It wasn't greasy and literally everything on the platter was delicious. So: Jasper Platter is a must! It doesn't sacrifice quality for quantity, so you get both and a bonus is the variety of things on it. Butter chicken with roti  was also nothing short of amazing. Loved it!

The ladies here are incredibly friendly. We tried the Egg Bara and the potato dish—both were absolutely delicious! We loved it so much, we came back again just to eat.
We also gave Yomari a try—it was fun and unique, though not quite our taste. Still, it was great to experience something traditional and different. Highly recommend stopping by if you're in the area!

Hidden gem of Kathmandu for food lovers!

I highly recommend trying absolutely everything…very inspiring, authentic, with a local atmosphere! The ladies in the kitchen are so lovely, and the food is amazing. Thank you!!

I found this place on Maps and I had to look around to find it as it is located in some small street between all the shops. They offer a couple of things and I tried them all. The ladies making the food and the people around me were super friendly and helped me very kindly. Although I didn't like the big white thing (Yomari) that much, 5 stars for this place!

Locals spot. was hard to find but worth it. Make sure you try both yomaris.
Clean food and served by very nice aunties who were happy to teach me the names of the food.

Indrachowk Bara Pasal is a hidden gem nestled near the bustling heart of Indrachowk. Famous for its authentic Newari delicacies like bara, yomari, and tarkari, this eatery has become a go-to spot for both locals and food enthusiasts alike. Owned and operated by a group of dedicated sisters, the place exudes a homely charm and serves freshly prepared Bara and Yomari at very reasonable prices.

Despite being a small and humble shop, it’s almost always busy, a testament to its popularity and the quality of its food. Whether you're craving a quick snack or looking to indulge in traditional flavors, this spot is well worth a visit. Come early if you want to avoid the rush.

Awesome food. Heads up, it’s a little hard to find- the restaurant is hidden behind another restaurant up a small, narrow staircase.

My dad and I were so glad we didn’t give up trying to find it because we really enjoyed our meal. We ordered several dishes to share, and the flavor was incredible. You can hear the chef making everything fresh in the kitchen, and each bite is bursting with flavor! Great way to taste traditional Newari food at a great price.

And the service was incredible! Very kind and welcoming. Best dining experience we had in Nepal!!

I kind of don’t want to write this review to prevent this restaurant from getting ruined by social media hypes. But this place is lovely. It’s a hidden gem that quite literally deserves that title. You have to look for the little alley with some restaurants just before the corner of the block. The food was so tasteful. We had fried momo’s, chatmari and for dessert yomari chaku. All really good. The owner helped us with choosing beteeen all options for the local dishes and the place is cute and intimite. Only a couple of tables. Definitely recommend.

Nice place, friendly staff.
Once we sat they asked us what we were looking for food wise, showed us the menu and gave suggestions.
Food amazing and good music

An absolute treasure.  Our food was so tasty, I am sad I didn't find this little bar/snack place earlier in my trip. We had one item that was off menu and it was great. It seemed to be on a rice flower crispy crepe and buff keema and, also fantastic.  I had a hot tea which I poured over my own ice. Very very good. I would highly recommend this place if you want to escape the busyness of the central city or just looking for a good feed.
Such a great lil hidden away gem. Food was delicious. Loved the Thukpa. Great service. Lovely atmosphere. Highly recommend

A fantastic lively restaurant with traditional live music and an indoor open fire. Great food, service and ambience. We shared king prawns, chicken dumplings and peri peri grilled chicken. All were delicious. Great wine, beer and cocktail list as well. The service was second to none with Om Ruwali and Lila Nath Rimal looking after us. A must do in Katmandu!

Awesome food here and great service. The mo mo dumplings are amazing. You can get them both inside a nice curry style broth or plain on a plate. We got both. Recommend grabbing an garlic naan bread to absorb the curry sauce. The chicken steak was incredible full of flavor and chicken was cooked and prepared to perfection. The Japanese style beef was a nice clean beef dish full of flavor also. Highly recommend eating here. Prices reasonable. Servers krishna and raju were great they were very fast and always just a quick wave away to order more food. Will be back

I’m honestly very selective of places to dine, but this one? nowhere more Nepali, home-like, and comforting

My family and I visited on New Year’s Day, and the experience was nothing short of wonderful. From the moment we walked in, the hospitality made us feel so welcomed and cared for. The food was absolutely delightful (the chicken chilly is a MUST) — rich in flavour, perfectly cooked, and full of that authentic, soulful taste that truly feels like home.
What stood out just as much was the extraordinary service; Mr. Dhakal and the staff were kind, attentive, and genuinely warm, easy to talk to which made the entire evening even more special. It was the perfect way to begin the new year !! and I’m more than happy to recommend this place to anyone looking for great food, heartfelt service, and a truly great dining experience.

I recently visited Kathmandu Grill Restaurant and Wine Bar and had a fantastic experience. The live music created such a great atmosphere and made the evening really special. The food was absolutely delicious, and as a vegan, I truly appreciated how accommodating and attentive they were to my dietary requirements.

The service was excellent from start to finish. A special thank you to Mahesh Rimal, who took exceptional care of us and made sure everything was perfect throughout our visit.

Highly recommend this place for great food, wonderful ambiance, and outstanding service!

Great experience at Kathmandu Grill. The service is always attentive and welcoming, with a special mention to Lila, the manager, who has looked after me since my first visit.

Food is delicious and consistent, loved the chicken ravioli with salad.

Nice atmosphere with live Nepali music, which really adds to the vibe. Definitely a place I keep coming back to.

address: United World Trade Centre, Tripura Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced—every cup feels like it’s made with real passion. Whether it’s a classic cappuccino or a specialty brew, the flavor is consistently outstanding.

address: United World Trade Centre, Tripura Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Food was very delicious🥰and after food we decided to try coffee and dessert 🍮 it was nice but for dessert don’t have much verities .. only 2 or 3 options for dessert… I wish have some more options for sweet 🥰staffs are very friendly and dedicated ❤️❤️❤️live music was amazing 🤩 🥰thank you so much for making our evening wonderful 🥰🥰(May 10)

address: United World Trade Centre, Tripura Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: I love the food, vibe and service of this place. Every thing tastes so good and the customer service is also topnotch.  Would highly suggest everyone to try tandoori and pastas.👍

address: United World Trade Centre, Tripura Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
I had an exceptional experience at this restaurant. The cocktails were outstanding—expertly crafted, well-balanced, and among the best I’ve had. The hookah was equally impressive, smooth and flavorful, adding a relaxing touch to the overall experience.
The food was delicious, with high-quality ingredients and excellent presentation. Every dish was satisfying and full of flavor. The service was attentive, friendly, and efficient, making the visit even more enjoyable.
Overall, this place offers a perfect combination of great drinks, quality food, and excellent service. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a memorable dining experience.

address: United World Trade Centre, Tripura Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Sundown by Karma offers an unforgettable dining experience with stunning views, Santorini-inspired decor, and a relaxing ambiance. The food was flavorful and delicious.The service was warm and attentive. A perfect spot for both casual dates, special occasions and family dinners.

address: Corner Point Restaurant And Bar, Pyukha Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: This restaurant has a warm and welcoming vibe, with friendly staff and a cozy ambience that makes dining enjoyable. The burger was juicy, flavorful, and definitely a highlight of the meal. Most of the food lived up to expectations, showcasing good quality and taste. However, the rolls felt a bit dry and the coffee could be stronger and more refined. With just a few improvements, especially on those fronts, this place has the potential to be a go-to spot for food lovers.

address: Corner Point Restaurant And Bar, Pyukha Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Tried the spaghetti and momo at Corner Point Restaurant and Bar, and both dishes were delicious! The flavors were spot on, and the portion sizes were just right. The ambiance was cozy, and the service was friendly and attentive. Overall, I had a really good experience and will definitely be visiting again soon

address: Corner Point Restaurant And Bar, Pyukha Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: The place is amazing and the atmosphere is great. But they opened a bottle of water without even asking and we had to pay Rs. 50. Other than that the food was delicious.

address: Corner Point Restaurant And Bar, Pyukha Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
I loved the Spaghetti Napolitana and spicy mango mojito they made!!!
The people were exceptional and polite.. I really loved having the food here!!!
The one thing is that the service was a little slow but again - they made it up on the food which was delicious!!!!
The place was also really nice with good vibes on the corner. Though it is on a crowded place Newroad gate, it is quiet and really comfortable. Not noisy at all!!! Will be visiting again soon!!! ❤️

address: Corner Point Restaurant And Bar, Pyukha Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
The food here was delicious , we ordered the meat lovers pizza and it was on top and so were the momo and chowmein . You know a restaurant is good when they can nail the basics and this one does it so well .
Thank you for the amazing food once again 😋

address: kwabahal, Wak Bajra Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: True to its name, a place for great breakfast. The ambience is spot on, a calming garden and the food has been fresh both the times we were here. The ingredients seemed fresh, the combinations well thought out and the dishes healthy.

address: kwabahal, Wak Bajra Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Fantastic breakfast! The Hollandaise breakfast shown in the picture was absolutely delicious and perfectly prepared. On top of that, the service from Bishow and his team was outstanding—very attentive, friendly, and welcoming. Highly recommended, we will definitely come back!

address: kwabahal, Wak Bajra Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: This place is a rare gem. Beautiful peaceful garden and the food is easily some of the best I have eaten in Thamel. I eat here one meal every day. The staff are very friendly and attentive. If you try once you will come back again and again. A lovely Oasis in Thamel!

address: kwabahal, Wak Bajra Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Lovely quiet restaurant off the beaten track. We had Authentic Nepalese food and it was very tasty. There was also a wide range of other cuisines on the menu. Divya looked after us and was very attentive and sweet.

address: kwabahal, Wak Bajra Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
I had a truly enjoyable experience at Breakfast House Thamel. The breakfast was delicious and the whole vibe of the place is chill and welcoming — perfect for solo travelers like me.

It’s the kind of spot where you can comfortably sit with a book, enjoy a quiet moment, or simply sip your tea and relax.

The service is also exceptional. Bishow, Divya, and Neha were always kind, attentive, and genuinely warm, which made every visit feel special.

Good ambience- outdoor for days when weather is nice - great for winters. Good mix of Arabian and Italian food. Been here multiple times - food is consistently good. Service is great. I had their classic hummus, couscous with veggies and shish touk- everything was good.

Great view from Tahina Terrace and nice breeze while enjoying a delicious pizza and desert.  Wish we had more time to try some other dishes because the menu looked awesome. Service was quick and we were even given an appetizer to try as a welcome. The chocolate flan with a layer of caramel had great texture and flavor.

Tahina Terrace is so good we ate there twice during our stay, and the menu is extensive enough that we had no difficulty ordering entirely different spreads each time. Clean and scenic, the staff are welcoming, attentive, and professionals when it comes to making guests feel at home (Sanjeev, Okesh, and Rabina were especially great). Highly recommend!

This restaurant served the perfect meal after our arduous trek. The Mediterranean cuisine was a lovely contrast to all the Nepalese food I’ve had throughout the trip.

The shish kebab, mushroom risotto, and calamari fritti were absolutely delicious. But the star of the night had to be the baba ganoush.

Really lovely meal in a nice restaurant situated in the Marriott Hotel Kathmandu, great views from the rooftop and a good escape from the hustle and bustle of Kathmandu’s streets!

The rooftop dine in place really serves an amazing food with super friendly employees Aayush & Rabina were best at the service . It was cold and they offered us throw over . It was such an amazing evening out there 🫶🏻

Loved the ambience. It's become one of my favorite spots.
I love everything about the place, no doubts. Food was delicious and the service was very good.
it was a good restaurant before but but noe the service and food has become worsee i ate pasta and now i am suffering from diarrhea
Good atmosphere with great service.

Food is below average... The service is quiet slow.. undoubtedly the prices are too expensive for the taste and the staff are really mannerless (specifically one staff was very impolite)... Not going back again

Good atmosphere
Food and services were so good
I am vegetarian so i can only give a veg food suggestions
Do must try paneer chilli 🌶
Fries periperi
Veg momo platter
Need to improve in veg waiwai  sadeko as tue crunchiness was not there

I recently visited Koyla Lounge and Bar and unfortunately had a disappointing experience. While having the chicken momos, I discovered what appeared to be an insect  in momos . This was very unexpected and concerning.
I informed the staff right away. Although they were polite, the situation really affected my confidence in the food hygiene standards. I hope the management takes this seriously and improves their quality checks, as the place has potential and the ambience is otherwise nice.

This longue is located at pipal bhot just 3 minute walk from newroad gate ready at the 8th floor.From the top we can enjoy the view of Basntapur and ratnapark as well as newly built Dharahara.The internal decoratation is not much decorated how ever it is satisfying.Have  comfortable sofas and cushions.I have visited at the day time so at that time there was no love music. Let's talk about the food , They tried to makes uniqueness on their food with a lot more food options. Overall i like the taste of food.Furthermore the  staff were quite polite and helpful. However the price of some  items are quite costlier it's doesn't satisfief the price. We have to pay service charge and 13℅vat this will increase the Bill amount by around 20℅ .
Worst experience. Had dragon chicken and chicken biryani. Dragon chicken was extremely sweet . And biryani was not even close to fried rice in terms of taste.

Amazing Experience!
Delicious food, great atmosphere, and excellent service! The Koyla lounge & bar was a must-try. Perfect for a cozy meal or a night out. Can’t wait to come back! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Great place to hangout with friends and family for all the occasions and celebrations. The food is great and quick service with reasonable price. I especially loved the live music. The staffs are really friendly.

We dined twice at Western Resto and can recommend it — 10/10.

The food was consistently delicious, freshly prepared, served hot, and in generous portions. The price-to-quality ratio is excellent, and the menu offers a great variety of options. The staff are friendly and attentive, making a great experience.

The open kitchen, visible through glass, appears professional, clean, and well-organized. Another big plus is the calm, pleasant atmosphere and the spotless facilities, including a Western-style toilet with toilet paper. We will definitely come again!

“This place is a true gem! From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with friendly smiles and quick service. The menu had a wide variety of options, and everything we tried tasted fresh and full of flavor. The highlight for me was the Pad Thai 😉. Definitely one of the best dining spots in thamel area.” Will visit again.😊

Must try the food here, delicious. Nepali Style mixed with Western Style. Nice combination.
Great service mind and good atmosphere.
👍🏻

I came for breakfast and it wasn't packed at all. The owners are very nice and the service is excellent.

The chicken fried rice is good! I was craving for asian food

We had amazing time in this cafe.
First night of our stay in Kathmandu we spent in their guest house. (It was good but noisy in the evening time because guests of the cafe downstairs stay loud until 11pm) so we had to move out the next day.

But we kept coming to the cafe almost every day during the next 2 weeks, for dinner and breakfast sometimes. Amazing food and service. Great cozy atmosphere. And we loved their live music night on Saturday. It was fusion music: sitar, guitar and tabla.

Thank you to the hole team for being always so professional and attentive to every guest. Thank you to the owner for creating such a great place where you want to come back.🙏

Great dining experience. Broad menu options include NOLA, Nepali, and Indian classics, including jambalaya, momo, dal bhat, and curries. Loads of cocktails, good beer selection, and NA drinks. Attentive staff. Fun interior decor, with covered and exposed seating options, fuses Nepali and NOLA styles.

Really good place with nepali arcitecture involvement. It lies in the tourism heart thamel.
The food is good too with quick service which is really appreciable.
I had ordered BLT sandwich which had really good portion of fries on the side with great balanced taste.
The staff there are really good too and i found them really friendly.

Totally recommend!

The choila Kathmandu beef was so tasty.. well marinated and spicy.. beyond expectations..

Despite the noise from the construction going on around, I believe it's very nice place to hangout

Stumbled upon New Orleans during my solo trip and it became one of my favorite spots. The vibe is super relaxed, with lovely music, good food, and a warm setup around the fire. It’s a great place to unwind, meet kind people, or just enjoy a peaceful evening. Tucked away in the street, but definitely worth finding. Thank you for the beautiful atmosphere!

Jhomsha Restaurant truly impressed me with its top-notch food, service, and atmosphere. The proprietor lady, her husband, and the service boy were all incredibly humble and welcoming, which made the experience even more special. Although they take some time to prepare the food, it is absolutely worth the wait and not disappointing at all. I’ve tested many places, and in my opinion, this is the best food in Thamel. They are also very helpful and attentive to guests. Highly recommended for anyone looking for great food and genuine hospitality.

Lively environment with very tasty food
Highly recommend for tibetan food❤️❤️❤️❤️ Buff tenderline grilled was amazing 😻

Cozy and esthetic restaurant near the heart of thamel. Always an amazing experience to be here.
Highly recommended for a date, friendly gatherings and family gatherings also. No doubt one of the best in Ktown.
A lovely chilled out, clean place with good, friendly service. The waiter recommended  Thenthuk Soup and I had the vegetarian version. Nice, subtle kick with a little lime. Tasty, thick noodles too.

Extremely beautiful space. Sofas and low tables,  spacious, two floors, veranda, nice lightening, good music. We ate food Tibetan and it was delicious: jhol paneer momos, ting mo and thukpa. Many vegetarian options.

We, a group of eight friends, recently visited Supper Club in Thamel to celebrate our farewell after completing our bachelor’s—and we had an absolutely amazing time!

The disco was incredible, featuring a lively Bollywood-themed ambience and energetic music that kept us dancing all night. The atmosphere truly made our celebration special and gave us memories we’ll always cherish.

The staff were very welcoming and friendly, making us feel comfortable throughout the evening. The drinks were well served, and everything was perfectly arranged.

Overall, it was a fantastic experience filled with fun, laughter, and great vibes. Highly recommend Supper Club for anyone looking to have an unforgettable night out in Thamel!

This club is a whole vibe! I was initially quite sceptical about going to a disco because I’m sensitive to loud noise, but I’m so glad I gave it a try. The atmosphere, music, and overall energy made it a night to remember. It was absolutely worth it, and I’d highly recommend experiencing it at least once — you might be pleasantly surprised, just like I was

One night I booked an underground supper club dinner in Cape Town — you literally eat in someone’s home or secret venue with strangers. It wasn’t fancy, but the energy was warm and the food honest. It felt more like joining friends for a meal than eating at a restaurant.
Food was so good and asthetic on point. I saw open kitchen where there's no age limit to work which is great initiative to collaborate with young gen and middle gen working  together.

The food here was alright, the service was great and the atmosphere was beautiful. Got the har gow and char siu noodles and they were pretty good. The har gow filling was very soft, almost too soft and mixed unlike traditional har gow with the whole shrimp filling. The char siu was good and well sauced.

Warm welcome by the whole team. Love the cosy lights. Love the food. Very light pizza dough. Best lasagna since a long time. Delicious soup.
I totally recommend this place ! Thanks everyone. The hotel is worth too. Hot shower. Wooden, comfortable and modern atmosphere. We feel good !

This is my third visit here at roadhouse. It’s my second time staying at Hotel Roadhouse and I will definitely keep coming back not only because of the delicious food but the warm hospitality of all the staffs here. Dinesh, Anish, and Saroj and many others at the cafe always made me feel super welcomed with their exceptional services. Definitely would recommend roadhouse cafe and hotel for anyone who’s traveling in Thamel, Nepal.

Had a really lovely time at Roadhouse Cafe. The place has a warm, cozy vibe that instantly makes you feel relaxed. The atmosphere is beautiful, especially in the evening—perfect for sitting back and enjoying good food with good company.

The food was delicious and well-presented. Every dish felt fresh and flavorful, clearly made with care. The menu has good variety, and the quality definitely matches the taste.

Service was decent and polite. The staff was friendly and helpful, though there’s always a little room for improvement in speed during busy hours.

Overall, it’s a great place to chill, click some beautiful pictures, and enjoy a peaceful café experience. Definitely worth visiting if you’re looking for good food, a nice ambience, and a calm, feel-good time. Would love to visit again 💛🍽️✨

Excellent pizza, bit different than what a italian would think of the ingredients, but very delicios.
The garden looked just so cozy and I could enjoy a nice time here.
Service is quite fast and people are welcoming

This is a very nice place to have breakfast lunch or dinner. We have come here 5 nights in a row and the menu is extremely varied. You have choice for very nice Western Food. There's is also vegetarian options available. ASTHA who was our waitress for all nights went over and beyond and she was very polite took orders with attention to detail and always confirmed with us the right order. I would highly recommend you come here for food. They also accept card payments which is a bonus.

Visited today for a quick snack. Veg momo was light and very delicious. Jhol was mouth watering sprinkled with lots of finely chopped coriander. Panner momo was also good. Very affordable. Would visit again.

I had heard a lot about this place, so I finally came to try it — and it was an amazing experience! The momo were fresh, clean, and full of delicious flavor. The staff are very young, energetic and friendly. I loved everything about this place. I truly recommend everyone to visit here at least once

I was new to Kathmandu. One day after moving to Kathmandu, I was wandering around and reached New Road. There, you can get such delicious Veg Mo:Mo: and Paneer. Everything seemed to be the best. It was delicious. ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️
Vegan Momo’s for only 100 NPR!  Fully vegan with chilli sauce on the side.  Fresh and tasty.  Close to Ratna Park bus terminal and Patan Square.  It’s a small place, very popular with locals.

I had heard a lot about this place from my friend, so today I went to try the momo. Unfortunately, I found something suspicious inside the momo.
I ordered 1 veg fry and 1 veg steam. While the momo was still hot, I split one into two pieces. While eating, I saw a long green rope-like thing that looked like a cabbage strip. When I tried to remove it, it felt different.
I asked the staff, “Dai, yo steam momo ma pareko, k ho yo?” He replied, “Bora ko pareko hola.” He replaced it with another plate, saying "sabai ma ta na hola", but I didn’t have the courage to risk my health again!

I request all momo lovers to eat carefully.
Thank you.

Gaya Restaurant is a great place to enjoy delicious food in a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The restaurant is well known for its tasty dishes made with fresh ingredients and careful preparation. The menu offers a good variety, with options to suit different tastes, including local and international flavors.

The staff are friendly, attentive, and always ready to help, which makes the dining experience comfortable and enjoyable. The restaurant is clean, nicely arranged, and has a relaxed ambiance—perfect for both casual meals and special occasions. Overall, Gaya Restaurant is appreciated for its quality food, good service, and pleasant environment, making it a place many guests are happy to visit again.

I am so happy to be at Gaia Restaurant I have been almost 25 countries the taste of Gaia is completely different than other the ambience cool and cozy very nice staff and friendly. They have a nice menu which is good for all age.  Offer-table price .
I recommend Gaia Restaurant.

Excellent Food, Drinks, and Outstanding Hospitality
I recently visited Gaia Restaurants Pvt. Ltd. and had a wonderful experience with both the food and drinks. Everything we ordered was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The drinks were equally impressive and very enjoyable.
A special mention goes to Tilak Shrestha, who served us throughout our visit. His hospitality was excellent, always welcoming us with a genuine smile and making sure we were comfortable. His attentive and friendly service truly made our experience even better.
We had a great time enjoying the food and drinks, and the overall atmosphere was warm and pleasant. Thank you to the team for such a lovely experience. Highly recommended, everything was great!
Great service, friendly and swift.  Diverse menu, local and international options. Food was top notch. Great place to return to after a long hike. Highly recommend

We have had really good meals and excellent service from the friendly and attentive staff! They cater for most choices, whether you enjoy your meat dishes, vegetarian or vegan.. A good place to stop in the Thamel area, that serves good, clean, and hygienic meals…

The tastiest food i have eaten since being nearly six weeks in Nepal. Absolutely delicious Thali. Good quality rice that was freshly cooked. Usually rice in Nepal is cheap quality, old, and reheated.
Really impresssed as i just passed by and it was empty, but it looked nice and gave it a try. Hope when I return its the same chef. Delicious accompanying sides. The best chutneys achar.
The only issue i saw was poor lonely goldfish who needs a friend so he is not alone. And the tank needs cleaning with more water filled up so he has more space to swim.
And smoking, although not allowed in katmandu, or Nepal, is allowed in this restaurant. The pollution from the street is awful, and the smoking as well is really bad
Today very bad experience. Waiting nearly TWO hours for food. No food arriving because the woman on the counter failed to tell the chef my same order as last time

Coffee and Snacks. Try this.
Very affordable and best for students and officials.
I love this place
Best

restaurant_name: Meraki Cafe,Restro & Bar | Restaurant in Thamel, Kathmandu
review_text: Total letdown. The food was greasy and tasted like old oil—nothing like the 'delicious' meals described in other reviews. The service setup is also a disaster. The staff stayed on a different floor, making it impossible to get any help without hunting them down. Save your money and go somewhere else.

restaurant_name: Meraki Cafe,Restro & Bar | Restaurant in Thamel, Kathmandu
review_text:
The restaurant is easily labeled to find. Even though the actual restaurant is an alley, a sign points in its direction, making the restaurant very hard to miss when you are looking for it.

Servers were ready to take our order as soon as we made our decisions and quickly got to work, making our orders.

There is a very wide selection of food in this restaurant, as seen in some of the pictures added.

The food came out quickly, relative to other places we have been to nearby. It took about 15 minutes for more simple orders and 20 minutes for complicated orders.

There was an issue with the pizza oven, which delayed my order, but they brought out a complimentary microwave pizza, which still tasted good, while I waited for my actual margherita pizza.

My group members commented on how good their food was, and even the complimentary food I was given tasted great.

The atmosphere is designed like an old building, but with the plants, lights, and music, everything fits together perfectly.
The location of the restaurant prevents customers from constantly hearing honking and cars driving by, further adding to the excellent atmosphere.

Overall, everything was excellent and very worth the cost.

restaurant_name: Meraki Cafe,Restro & Bar | Restaurant in Thamel, Kathmandu
review_text:
Meraki Cafe, Restro & Bar is truly one of the best restaurants in Kathmandu! Located at Narsingh Chowk Marg, just a short walk from Thamel, this place is a must-visit for anyone searching for a top restaurant in Kathmandu with great food, amazing ambiance, and outstanding service.

If you’re looking for the best restaurant in Thamel, Meraki Cafe, Restro & Bar should be at the top of your list. The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, the staff is friendly and attentive, and the menu offers a fantastic variety of delicious dishes and refreshing drinks. Whether you’re visiting for breakfast, lunch, dinner, or cocktails, this restaurant in Thamel delivers a wonderful dining experience.

The food quality, presentation, and overall vibe truly make it stand out as one of the best restaurants in Kathmandu. I highly recommend Meraki Cafe, Restro & Bar to both locals and tourists looking for a memorable dining experience in the heart of the city. Can’t wait to visit again!

restaurant_name: Meraki Cafe,Restro & Bar | Restaurant in Thamel, Kathmandu
review_text: If you are looking for the best restaurant in Kathmandu, Meraki Cafe, Restro & Bar is an absolute must-visit. Tucked away in Narsingh Chowk Marg, it’s a peaceful escape from the busy streets. I’ve tried many places, but this is hands down the best restaurant in Thamel for authentic Turkish cuisine and a relaxing vibe. Whether you need a cozy restaurant in Kathmandu for a date or a lively restaurant in Thamel to hang out with friends, the food quality and service here are unmatched. Highly recommend the mezze platters!

restaurant_name: Meraki Cafe,Restro & Bar | Restaurant in Thamel, Kathmandu
review_text: Incredibly authentic and tasty food served up by a very well-trained staff in a comfortable environment. So many flavors brought together on an immaculately-arranged plate. The half-Turkish chef introduced himself and I could see how proud he was of his work spreading the delights of Turkish food through Nepal. Highly recommended, 10 out of 10. One of my top 3 favorite restaurants in Kathmandu.

address: Trisara Building, दरवार मार्ग, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Sanchyan Ramen has always been a go to for good ramen, and this time we visited their Durbar Marg outlet. The space is bigger and feels warmer, with a cozy setup that works well for a solo date or even for couples.

We usually order the Spicy Pork Ramen, and it never disappoints. The spice level is just right if you enjoy a bit of heat without it being overwhelming. The chashu pork practically melts in your mouth, and the eggs are perfectly soft boiled, not overcooked and not too runny. This place really serves a comforting, satisfying bowl every single time.

address: Trisara Building, दरवार मार्ग, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: We tried the Rainbow Roll, California Roll, Miso Ramen, and Teriyaki Donburi. The food was really delicious — the Rainbow Roll was definitely the highlight! The dishes could be served a bit hotter, especially on colder days, and the Miso Ramen could use a little more depth in flavor. Overall, it’s a must-visit spot for Japanese food lovers! 🍣🍜

address: Trisara Building, दरवार मार्ग, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text: We really loved the food and service here. The chef personally came to our table to ask about the meal, and we really loved that. We ate various ramen, sushi, and tempura dishes. All of them were tasty and looked aesthetic. I also liked the enclosed Japanese-style private room, and I appreciated them showing us Japanese culture by welcoming us and saying goodbye in Japanese.

address: Trisara Building, दरवार मार्ग, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
We were spontaneously looking for lunch for our daughter and stumbled upon this lovely restaurant. We were the first guests and were kindly offered a private table. The food was moderately priced and, fortunately, everyone enjoyed it.

Here is a small selection of our dishes

address: Trisara Building, दरवार मार्ग, काठमाडौँ 44600, Nepal
review_text:
I along with my family we went to visit Sanchyan Ramen at Durbar Marg which is a Japanese restaurant.

From the moment we stepped in, the staff welcomed us with warm hospitality, which we truly appreciated. The service throughout our visit was friendly and attentive.

We ordered the Chin Ramen and Avocado Sushi, and both dishes were delicious. The ramen, in particular, lived up to its reputation — rich in flavor and incredibly satisfying. The sushi was fresh and perfectly balanced.

Overall, we had a wonderful time at Sanchyan Ramen. The cozy atmosphere, great food, and heartfelt service made it a memorable family outing. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Japanese cuisine in the heart of Kathmandu.

address: The Park, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Le Mirch at The Park, Durbar Marg is a solid pick if you’re craving Indian food. The flavors were good overall; naan was fresh and satisfying, though the tandoori felt a bit dry.

They also offered a complimentary whiskey-based cocktail, which was a nice touch. I went with my family and we had a good time. If you’re in the area and want Indian food, it’s worth a visit.

address: The Park, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
OMG guys, this place deserves an 11/10! 😭✨
It was raining that night and we were craving Jhol Momo, even though it wasn’t on the menu. But the staff were so kind and accommodating—they actually made it for us, and honestly, it was one of the best Jhol Momos I’ve ever had!
The hospitality was amazing, the food was absolutely worth it, and the best part—it’s halal too. Highly, highly recommended!

address: The Park, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Had a great experience at Le Mirch. The vibe of the place is warm and welcoming!

The staff was super helpful and attentive throughout our visit, making the experience even better.

The food was absolutely delicious. Every dish was packed with flavour, and the eggplant curry truly stole the show today.

Highly recommend giving this spot a try if you’re craving authentic Indian food done right.

address: The Park, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Excellent Indian food ! Limited menu but whatever is there ...it's excellent ooooooo ! You should see the pictures I have shared all my plates are empty .. it's that delicious.. & the service 100000/100 it's that good !

address: The Park, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
At first glance the restaurant seems lovely, with vibrant decor and convenient location. However, once the food actually came it was clear that not really CHINESE food, rather chinese inspired. It was like the chef had taken a glance at chinese food and tried to replicate its look and look only. Furthermore although the restaurant clearly places an emphasis on Sichuan flavors through its menu I felt that the food was not up to par and did not reflect this.

I suppose if you were just looking for a photo spot or are not particularly a fan of chinese food this place would do.

address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Awesome Hard Rock Cafe! I went to this restaurant for my birthday and I felt like a VIP. I met the manager, Pukar, and we had a very nice conversation. He was friendly and enjoyable to talk to. I had the buffalo burger and it was great. I did not have buffalo before, so it was the first time for me. The bartender was also friendly and courteous. You must visit this Hard Rock when you are a Kathmandu.

address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Three visits and counting. This might be my favorite Hard Rock location anywhere.

The staff here are incredible. Our 10-month-old got the rock star treatment every time we walked in.

Our server Akrosh helped us work around food allergies, checked with the kitchen himself, gave us tips on local sights to visit, and arranged our taxi home.

Caught a local band on New Year’s Eve doing AC/DC and Bon Jovi. Place was packed.

Burgers are buffalo, not beef (beef is prohibited in Nepal). Delicious.

If you’re in Thamel, don’t skip this one.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
I have visited Hard Rock Cafe 2–3 times with different friends, and every visit has been fantastic. The ambience is vibrant and lively, making it a perfect spot to hang out. The service is consistently great, with friendly and attentive staff.

The café is spacious, giving plenty of room to relax and enjoy without feeling crowded. Overall, it’s a go-to place for good food, excellent service, and a fun atmosphere. Highly recommended!

address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text:
Visited Hard Rock Cafe at Durbarmarg with high expectations, but came away with mixed feelings. The location is great—right in the heart of Kathmandu, with easy access and a lively atmosphere. The interior is stylish and carries the brand’s signature rock-n-roll vibe, which definitely adds to the experience.

The hospitality was genuinely warm and attentive. The staff were friendly and courteous, and the service overall was smooth and professional. That was definitely a highlight of the visit.

However, I was a bit underwhelmed when it came to the food. While the presentation was good, the flavors didn't quite match the price tag. It felt overpriced for the portion size and taste. Maybe I had higher expectations, given the global brand name, but the dishes just didn’t deliver that wow factor for me.

If you're going for the vibe, music, drinks, or just to hang out with friends, this place is worth checking out. But if you're looking for great value dining, especially in terms of food, there are better options around Durbarmarg.

address: Hard Rock Cafe, Sherpa Mall, Durbar Marg, Kathmandu 44600, Nepal
review_text: Such a great energy behind the bar.Shanks was Super welcoming, quick service,and recommended me a perfect drinks.Made the whole night even better.Definitely coming back.
I absolutely love this place! The food is always delicious, the service is super fast, and the overall vibe is great. Definitely one of my favorite spots to visit. Highly recommended!

I have been their fan and loyal customer when they were cloud kitchen . There burgers is best in town try it once and you will not be disappointed. It is big and juicy and has their own sauce. I find it disappointing if they don’t have their own sauce and taste . This place surly knows their sauce game cause it is addicting.
My favorite is spicy fried chicken and pulled pork . Their nachos is also good . Try their burgers one time and you will be addicted for life time . 😜
This place owners are also very nice and friendly to the customers .
